Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(uv): Support PEP 735 include-group syntax in dependency-groups (#843)

Follow-up to
https://github.com/aspect-build/rules_py/issues/841#issuecomment-4027124653

PEP 735 allows dependency groups to reference other groups via the
`{include-group = "other-group"}` syntax. This commit adds support for
parsing and resolving these references in the uv.project extension.

The implementation:
- Adds a shared `resolve_dependency_group_specs()` helper in
dep_groups.bzl
- Uses iterative expansion (Starlark doesn't support recursion)
- Detects circular references and provides clear error messages
- Preserves the order of specs when expanding include-groups

Example usage:
```toml
[dependency-groups]
build = ["setuptools"]
test = ["pytest"]
dev = [
    {include-group = "build"},
    {include-group = "test"},
]
```

Includes E2E test case in e2e/cases/uv-include-group/.

---

### Changes are visible to end-users: yes

- Searched for relevant documentation and updated as needed: yes
- Breaking change (forces users to change their own code or config): no
- Suggested release notes appear below: no

### Test plan

- New test cases added
- Manual testing (using the internal codebase where I hit this + a
`local_path_override` pointing rules_py at my fork with these changes)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Consolidate city pages and extract shared constants (#123)

## Summary
- **9 city service pages → 1 dynamic route** (`[city].astro` +
`service-cities.ts`), eliminating ~2,500 lines of near-identical code
- **Shared constants** (`site.ts`) centralizing `BASE_URL`, contact
info, person schema refs across 15+ files
- **Shared utilities** (`tagSlug`, `stripExt`, `getBookingMonth`,
`formatDate`) extracted from 4+ duplicated locations
- **GamePage wrapper** for arcade game boilerplate, reducing each game
entry from 42 to 6 lines

## Test plan
- [x] `npm run lint` — 0 errors (2 pre-existing warnings)
- [x] `npm run build` — 39 pages built successfully
- [ ] Verify all 9 city pages render correctly at `/services/{city}/`
- [ ] Verify arcade games still load at `/arcade/descent/` and
`/arcade/gridlock/`
- [ ] Spot-check JSON-LD structured data on city and portfolio pages

commit.message:
fix(runtime): lazy async lock init and FAISS dimension validation

asyncio.Lock() at module level binds to wrong/missing event loop on
Python 3.10+ (DeprecationWarning) and 3.12+ (RuntimeError). Now
lazily created inside get_async_llm_client() on first call.

VectorStore.add_documents() and .search() now validate vector
dimensions against index.d before calling FAISS, converting cryptic
C++ segfaults into clear ValueError messages.

4 regression tests added, all 317 tests passing.

commit.message:
Fix packet generation: use correct column name hash_sha256 and add RPC for commit

The commit route was failing silently because the code used sha256 but the
actual database column is hash_sha256. Added a commit_version RPC function
to bypass PostgREST schema cache issues, and normalized the column name
in the packet route.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Accumulate partial close P&L across days to prevent data loss

Previously, each day's trades_realized replaced the partial entry,
losing previous days' accumulated P&L. Now stores base_pnl in the
entry_price field so it survives same-day updates and server restarts.

When a position fully closes, uses accumulated total (base + today's
trades) instead of Zerodha's pos.pnl which is only the day P&L.

commit.message:
Fix RPi daemon not exiting on BT serial disconnect, leaking child processes

The main loop continued on read errors indefinitely, holding /dev/rfcomm0
and preventing rfcomm watch from accepting new connections. Also, wl-copy
processes spawned by typeTextPaste were never tracked or killed on shutdown.

- Break main loop after 3 consecutive read errors for clean shutdown
- Unify cleanup in main loop exit (close stopCh, injector, port)
- Simplify signal handler to just close port, triggering loop exit
- Track wl-copy/xclip processes in Injector and kill on Close()

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix model.half() error: use model.model for PPLMetric evaluation

Model is a wrapper class; the actual HF model is model.model.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix event loop blocking in gather — offload sync HTTP to threads

gather() called _fetch_one() directly for cockpit/commerce/http sources,
which use sync urllib internally. When called from async context this
blocked the event loop for up to 18s, causing cascading cockpit API
timeouts (25% error rate in exp8 18h run).

Wrap IO-bound sources in asyncio.to_thread() so sync HTTP runs in the
default executor without blocking the event loop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: strict mode — any BLOCK wins, only USER can override to safe

The RxJS pipeline ran ML and Haiku in parallel. When Haiku (higher priority)
incorrectly returned SAFE, it overrode ML's correct BLOCK. This violated
the strict mode principle: false positives are OK, false negatives are not.

Now: once any source says BLOCK, the image stays blocked. Only a USER-level
manual flag (priority 4) can override a block back to safe. This prevents
Haiku's occasional false negatives from showing images of women.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Clarify budget consumption in prioritization prompts and tool descriptions

The prioritization LLM didn't understand that each scout round costs 1 budget
unit, leading it to set max_rounds far above available budget (e.g. max_rounds=5
on budget=2). Explicit budget accounting in the prompt, tool descriptions, and
field descriptions fixes this.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: re-analyze background-image elements when URL changes (Taboola placeholder swap)

Taboola ads first set a tiny data:image/png placeholder as background-image,
which the extension correctly classifies as safe. When Taboola later swaps in
the real image URL, the MutationObserver was skipping the re-analysis because
the element was already marked safe.

Now tracks the background-image URL via data-se-last-bg-url and re-analyzes
when it changes. Also fixes flushMutationBatch to check the added element
itself (not just descendants) via querySelectorAll.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: critical base58_decode stack overflow corrupting SOL send recipient address (v0.9.27) [BUILD]

base58_decode() wrote a null terminator past the decoded data, causing a
stack buffer overflow when the output buffer was exactly 32 bytes (Solana
pubkeys/blockhashes). In sol_tx_send_lamports(), the blockhash decode
overwrote to_pubkey[0] (0xd1→0x00), silently sending funds to wrong address.

Fixes:
- Remove null terminator from base58_decode (binary data, not string)
- Use temp buffer in sol_rpc_get_recent_blockhash as defense-in-depth
- Fix SOL RPC params memory leak (ownership transfer to sol_rpc_call)
- Fix CLI wallet balance "Not initialized" (ensure_wallets_loaded)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: session resume hang from blocking stdin read (#127)

* fix: session resume hang from blocking stdin read

_read_hook_input() called sys.stdin.read() which blocks until EOF.
When Claude Code does not close the stdin pipe for SessionStart
resume/compact hooks, this caused an infinite hang at "resuming
session."

Two fixes:
1. Add select() with 100ms timeout to _read_hook_input() so it
   returns immediately when no stdin data is available.
2. Remove _read_hook_input() from four handlers that never used the
   data: session-start, session-resume, session-end, stop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use os.read with select loop instead of blocking sys.stdin.read

Addresses Copilot and Bugbot review: sys.stdin.read() blocks until
EOF even after select() returns readable.  Now uses os.read() in a
select loop with 50ms inter-chunk timeout — never requires EOF.

Adds 5 tests for _read_hook_input covering: empty stdin, valid JSON,
no-EOF pipe (regression test), no-data open pipe, invalid JSON.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
